reforms.description: A new constitutional provision, the 'debt containment rule', was adopted to cap public expenditure and maintain a balanced structural budget, allowing deficits during recessions while requiring surpluses during economic upswings.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to ensure fiscal stability, which can support long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The provision has been adopted but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform introduces a significant structural change in public finance management.
reforms.source_quote: A new constitutional provision –the 'debt containment rule'– was adopted by the Parliament in June 2001.

reforms.description: The inter-cantonal agreement on university financing was updated to introduce differentiated compensation for students across various fields of study, addressing the costs incurred by cantons hosting universities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing the financial disparities in university funding, the reform aims to enhance the quality of education and research, which can contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The agreement has been updated and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The update represents a significant adjustment to the existing funding framework for universities.
reforms.source_quote: In 1998, a new agreement was implemented, introducing a differentiation in compensation among three groups of subjects.
